feedback.text: Dear European Commission, As many others have already stated, the video game industry is in dire need of proper and strict regulation regarding the handling and sale of online-only games. These games can be shut down at any given time without warning or end-of-life support, leaving customers with a note that the servers have been shut down and suggesting they move to the newest installments of the game, which can also be shut down at the publishers discretion, on any random day. Such behaviour must be regulated, as it undermines the fundamental principles of trade. Thank you for your time.
